---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header container">
  <p class="page-header-label">Work</p>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">Projects</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">
    Applied research, teaching tools, and data storytelling experiments.
    Each project is built around a real methodological question.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="project-grid container">

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Data Storytelling · Teaching Module</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Hip-Hop Periodic Table</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      A fully documented dataset of 173 hip-hop acts — 130 solo artists and 43
      groups — scored across five lyricism dimensions, with per-artist confidence
      ratings and a published data dictionary. Built to teach measurement validity,
      sampling bias, composite scoring, and uncertainty communication — using a
      subject that provokes genuine debate.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Excel / readxl</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <a href="../hiphop/hiphop_periodic_table.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#8C6A8D;">
      Explore the periodic table →
    </a>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Data Storytelling · Teaching Module</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">The Barnum Test</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      Does your personality type measure anything at all? A 90-second interactive
      exercise builds a personalized profile, asks how accurate it feels, then
      reveals that every participant receives the identical profile — and that the
      answers were never used. A clear, memorable lesson in construct validity.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Construct validity</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <a href="../barnum/barnum_test.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#8C6A8D;">
      Take the Barnum Test →
    </a>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Applied Research · Criminal Justice</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Counted Wrong: Emerging Adults &amp; the Crossroads Study</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      A close analysis of longitudinal data from the Crossroads Study — following
      young people through their first contact with the justice system — to illustrate
      the complexity of where adolescence ends and adulthood begins. The legal system
      draws a bright line at age 18, but human development does not. A project about
      what happens when a measured boundary is treated as a biological one.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>Longitudinal data</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Developmental measurement</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Law &amp; policy</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Applied Research · Aging &amp; the Law</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">A Right That Exists on Paper: Compassionate Release</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      Nearly every state and the federal system has a compassionate or medical
      release law on the books — a legal mechanism to release people who are dying,
      gravely ill, or simply too old to pose a risk. Almost none are used.
      Drawing on a fifty-state review and the data behind <em>Aging and the Criminal
      Legal System</em> (APA, 2025), this project measures the distance between a
      law's <em>presence</em> and its <em>utilization</em> — and asks what an unused
      statute reveals about how a system measures mercy.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>50-state legal review</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Aging &amp; corrections</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Law &amp; policy</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Data Storytelling · Teaching Module</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">How Old Is Old?</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      A 60-second exercise: set a slider to the age at which someone becomes
      "old," "a senior," "too old to drive," or "too old to start over." The
      responses reveal how little consensus exists — and how the law fixes hard
      numbers (18, 50, 65) onto a boundary with no basis in nature. A study in
      operationalization, and a companion to <em>Counted Wrong</em> at the other
      end of the lifespan.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Operationalization</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Data Storytelling · Teaching Module</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Trust Your Gut?</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      Review a set of realistic case files and predict who returns to court and
      who does not. Your accuracy is then scored against a simple two-variable
      statistical rule that consistently outperforms the reader — a pointed
      demonstration in applied prediction that expert intuition routinely loses
      to a checklist.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Actuarial vs. clinical</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </div>

</div>

<p style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 0 1rem; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.9rem;">
More projects in progress.
</p>
```
